The IOC summary confirms no network activity (no IPs, domains, or HTTP), which aligns with the initial overview. There is 1 registry persistence key, 2 dropped files, and 6 processes created. The high

The dropped files are 'avscan.exe' in Temp (likely the main payload or a renamed copy) and 'hosts.exe' in Windows directory (suspicious name, likely a dropper or secondary payload). The initial overvi

The registry changes confirm a single persistence mechanism: adding 'avscan.exe' to the Run key under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run. This ensures the malware executes on user logi

The process activity confirms the execution chain: the main exe spawns WScript.exe to run 'W_X_C.vbs', which likely executes the 'W_X_C.bat' file. It also uses ntvdm.exe (16-bit compatibility layer) a
